  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Confirm cleanup scope: A) language-level copyedit only (grammar/clarity/concision) or B) substantive content cleanup resolving conceptual and logical inconsistencies and aligning claims with described mechanisms/results (with light structural tweaks)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft’s central claims and positioning likely need reframing to reconcile the contribution statement with the described approach.",
    "R1.2": "There are internal contradictions between stated progress properties and the mechanism descriptions that must be logically reconciled.",
    "R1.3": "Reported performance and scaling claims should align precisely with the experimental setup and numbers presented.",
    "R1.6": "Cleaning up typically entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments.",
    "RX.1": "Given the ambiguous request, any assumptions about intended scope or interpretation should be made explicit.",
    "RX.2": "Strong assertions about guarantees and superiority should be calibrated to avoid overstating what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the scope of cleaning the provided text rather than redesigning algorithms or adding new evidence.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding misleading statements about concurrency guarantees helps prevent downstream engineering harm.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should preserve clear linkage between claims, sections, and reported results to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Confirm cleanup scope: A) language-level copyedit only (grammar/clarity/concision) or B) substantive content cleanup resolving conceptual and logical inconsistencies and aligning claims with described mechanisms/results (with light structural tweaks)?

rationale.R1.1: The draft’s central claims and positioning likely need reframing to reconcile the contribution statement with the described approach.
rationale.R1.2: There are internal contradictions between stated progress properties and the mechanism descriptions that must be logically reconciled.
rationale.R1.3: Reported performance and scaling claims should align precisely with the experimental setup and numbers presented.
rationale.R1.6: Cleaning up typically entails sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments.
rationale.RX.1: Given the ambiguous request, any assumptions about intended scope or interpretation should be made explicit.
rationale.RX.2: Strong assertions about guarantees and superiority should be calibrated to avoid overstating what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the scope of cleaning the provided text rather than redesigning algorithms or adding new evidence.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding misleading statements about concurrency guarantees helps prevent downstream engineering harm.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should preserve clear linkage between claims, sections, and reported results to maintain traceability.
